Question: A 4.0-kg particle moves in a circle of radius 2.0 m. The angular momentum of the particle varies in time according to $$l = 5.0 t^{2} .$$ (a) What is the torque on the particle about the center of the circle at $$t = 3.4 \text{s}$$ ? (b) What is the angular velocity of the particle at $$t = 3.4 \text{s}$$ ?
o3 Answer:
Data  
 mass m = 4.0 kg  
 radius r = 2.0 m  
 angular momentum L(t) = 5.0 t² ( SI units: kg m² s⁻¹ )

(a)  Torque about the centre  

Torque τ is the time–rate of change of angular momentum:  
 τ = dL/dt

dL/dt = d(5.0 t²)/dt = 10 t

At t = 3.4 s:  
 τ = 10 (3.4) = 34 kg m² s⁻² = 34 N·m

(b)  Angular velocity at t = 3.4 s  

For a particle of mass m moving in a circle of radius r, the magnitude of angular momentum is  
 L = I ω = (m r²) ω

Hence  
 ω = L / (m r²)

At t = 3.4 s  
 L = 5.0 (3.4)² = 5.0 (11.56) = 57.8 kg m² s⁻¹  
 m r² = 4.0 (2.0)² = 4.0 (4) = 16 kg m²

Thus  
 ω = 57.8 / 16 ≈ 3.6 rad s⁻¹

Answers  
(a) τ ≈ 34 N·m  
(b) ω ≈ 3.6 rad/s
